source: NvChad - Overview: the technologies behind the IDE: Lua, the Language Server Protocol, tree-sitter

questions.q: The name 'Lua' comes from the Portuguese word meaning...
questions.why: Lua means 'moon' in Portuguese; it was developed in Brazil.

questions.q: How is Lua code actually executed?
options: The interpreter compiles it to bytecode, which the Lua virtual machine runs
It is interpreted line-by-line straight from the .lua file
It is compiled to native machine code by GCC
It runs inside a web browser
questions.why: Unlike Python, Lua compiles to bytecode run by the Lua VM.

questions.q: Lua is most often used as...
options: an embedded/integrated scripting language inside other programs
questions.why: Lua is rarely stand-alone; it is embedded (games, Nmap, ModSecurity, etc.).

questions.q: Under which license is Lua distributed?
questions.why: Lua is free/open-source under the permissive MIT license.

questions.q: What is the Language Server Protocol (LSP)?
options: A standard so one language server can give autocomplete/goto-definition to many editors
questions.why: LSP standardizes tool<->server communication so servers are reusable across editors.

options: Parses source into a syntax tree and updates it incrementally on each change
questions.why: Tree-sitter is a parser generator plus incremental parsing library.

questions.q: tree-sitter helps improve which of these?
options: syntax highlighting, code navigation, and refactoring
questions.why: A syntax tree lets the editor manipulate code structure intelligently.
